zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Agnes  Baroness of Basing Wiotus  Wigton Wyatt於1320年出生在英國越可爾時，她的父親Odard Wigton de Norwoods16歲，母親Millicent Blakehill12歲。她和Adam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyatt至少有6個兒子。她去世於1384年在英國越可爾諾武德 在 費福斯頓享年64歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可斯特普尼聖 登斯坦 安德 哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Agnes  Baroness of Basing Wiotus  Wigton Wyatt于1320年出生在英国越可爾时，她的父亲Odard Wigton de Norwoods16岁，母亲Millicent Blakehill12岁。她和Adam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyatt至少有6个儿子。她去世于1384年在英国越可爾諾武德 在 費福斯頓享年64岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可斯特普尼聖 登斯坦 安德 哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Wigton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Glasgow): habitational name from Wigton (Cumberland), named with the Old English personal name Wicga + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. It is unclear whether the Scottish name is of independent origin, but it does not seem to be associated with Wigtown (Wigtownshire).

nameMeanings.name: Wyatt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal names Wiot or Wiet. These are often pet forms of Wy, from ancient Germanic Wido (see Guy , compare Wyand 1). Wy is the usual Norman French form of the name; the Parisian French form is Guy. Wiot was also used as a diminutive of William . As a surname Wyot(t) became Wyatt during the 16th century.

in Suffolk, this is sometimes a variant of Wyard, from the Middle English personal name Wiard, which is generally from Old English Wīgheard, which is composed of the elements wīg ‘battle’ + heard ‘bold, brave’. Occasionally it may be from Old French Wiard, ancient Germanic Withard (see Viard ).

variants.name: Wyeth
meanings: English (Hampshire): perhaps a variant of   Wyatt  .
History: Nicholas Wyeth emigrated from Suffolk, England to Cambridge, MA, before 1645. John Wyeth (1770–1858) was born in Cambridge and became a prominent publisher and editor in Harrisburg, PA.

variants.name: Guy
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from a common Old French personal name Guy, from ancient Germanic Wido, a short form of compound names based on the element wid(u) ‘wood, forest’, Latinized as Guido .
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, an occupational name for a guide, Old French gui (a derivative of gui(d)er ‘to guide’, of ancient Germanic origin).

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Baroness
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Norwegian: old variant of Barsnes (and, in North America, probably also an altered form of this), a habitational name from a farmstead so named in the county of Sogn og Fjordane, possibly from a compound of the Old Norse personal name Baldrekr + nes ‘point, headland’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Basing
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Basing in Hants.

2 English: relationship name occasionally perhaps from the Middle English personal name Basing or Besing, perhaps an Old English pet form of Old Scandinavian Bassi or Bessi (a recognized pet-form, via bersi, of bjǫrn ‘bear’), or perhaps Old English Bassa. Apart from the 1202 example, the following bearers may alternatively belong with (1).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Agnes  Baroness of Basing Wiotus  Wigton Wyatt was born in 1320, in Yorkshire, England, her father, Odard Wigton de Norwoods, was 16 and her mother, Millicent Blakehill, was 12. She had at least 6 sons with Adam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyatt. She died in 1384, in Norwood in Fewston, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, England.

Elle a eu au moins 6 fils avec Adam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyatt. Elle est décédée en 1384, à Norwood in Fewston, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes  Baroness of Basing Wiotus  Wigton Wyattが1320年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Odard Wigton de Norwoodsは16歳，母Millicent Blakehillは12歳であった。彼女はAdam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyattとの間に少なくとも6人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1384年にイギリスヨークシュアイアノーウッド イン フエウストンで64歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスステプニーサイント ダンスタン アンド アル サインツ チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St. Dunstan, Stepney, Middlesex, England: 영국 미들레세스 스테프니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes  Baroness of Basing Wiotus  Wigton Wyatt(이)가 1320년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Odard Wigton de Norwoods(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Millicent Blakehill(은)는 12세였다. 그녀는 Adam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyatt(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1384년 영국 요르크실르 노우드 인 프w스턴에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 스테프니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 처르치에 묻혔다.

Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos com Adam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyatt. ru.St. Dunstan, Stepney, Middlesex, England: сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Agnes  Baroness of Basing Wiotus  Wigton Wyatt родилась в 1320 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Odard Wigton de Norwoods было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Millicent Blakehill, 12. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей от Adam "The Admiral" "Wiotus" Wyatt. Она умерла в 1384 г. в местности норвуд ин фюстон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия.
